name: Gravity
description: A persistent acceleration field pulls bodies along a defined vector and shapes jumps, falls, arcs, and physical weight.
design_notes: Gravity is the baseline contract for how the world treats airborne bodies. Designers should define the gravity_acceleration_vector, per-body gravity_scale, terminal velocity caps, and any local field override rules before tuning jumps, knockback, buoyancy, or moving-platform transfer.
design_purpose: Give motion a readable downward pull and make airborne decisions have commitment.
Shape traversal feel through jump arcs, fall recovery, ledge timing, and object motion.
Provide a shared physical rule that other mechanics can modify rather than each inventing its own fall behavior.
player_fantasy: The player feels the world has weight and learns to master arcs, landings, and falling risk.

parameters.name: gravity_acceleration_vector
parameters.description: Direction and magnitude applied to affected bodies each fixed simulation step.

parameters.name: gravity_scale_multiplier
parameters.description: Per-body multiplier used for characters, projectiles, debris, or special zones.

parameters.name: terminal_velocity_cap
parameters.description: Maximum falling velocity allowed before integration clamps additional acceleration.

parameters.name: fixed_step_policy_id
parameters.description: Simulation cadence rule used to keep fall arcs stable across frame rates and platforms.

parameters.name: local_field_priority
parameters.description: Priority used when overlapping gravity zones compete to modify the same physics_body_id.

edge_cases: A body crosses from world gravity into a local field while airborne and keeps stale terminal velocity settings.
A jump, knockback, or dash overrides vertical velocity during the same fixed step that gravity is integrated.
Large frame spikes cause multiple fixed steps and make fall damage or landing events fire more than once.
Network rollback restores position but not accumulated vertical velocity, producing a different landing frame.
common_bugs: Gravity is applied in both Update and the fixed simulation loop, doubling fall acceleration on high frame rates.
Character controllers and rigidbodies use different gravity_scale_multiplier values and separate jump tuning tables.
Terminal velocity clamps upward motion as well as falling motion because the velocity sign is not checked.
Local gravity zones change the vector but leave camera, input, or contact_normal handling in world-down assumptions.
balancing_notes: Stronger gravity can make jumps feel crisp, but it narrows correction windows for players with slower reactions.
Terminal velocity should match level scale and fall recovery; very high caps make pits feel punitive and collision tunneling more likely.
If gravity changes by zone, surface language and camera orientation must help players predict the new fall direction.
accessibility_notes: Offer assists such as wider coyote windows, jump buffering, or reduced fall penalty when gravity tuning demands fast reactions.
Use clear environmental cues for altered gravity fields so players are not surprised by sudden motion changes.

implementation_notes.unity: For Rigidbody objects, set Physics.gravity only for global rules and use per-body acceleration for local fields or character-specific gravity_scale_multiplier. CharacterController movement should integrate gravity_acceleration_vector in the same fixed cadence as collision sweeps, and Netcode snapshots need position, velocity, gravity_scale, and active field id.
implementation_notes.godot: ProjectSettings gravity works for global 2D or 3D defaults, but custom CharacterBody movement should store vertical velocity and gravity_scale explicitly. Area2D or Area3D zones can override gravity vectors; debug draw active field priority and contact normals to catch transition errors.
implementation_notes.unreal: CharacterMovement exposes GravityScale and movement modes for most character cases, while physics bodies can receive custom acceleration through components or Physics Volumes. Replicated games should keep server authority over gravity mode changes and include velocity plus movement mode in correction packets.
implementation_notes.web: In Phaser, Matter.js, or a custom loop, integrate gravity from a fixed-step accumulator rather than render delta. Store physics_body_id, velocity, gravity_scale, and active zone in rollback snapshots so replay and browser frame spikes produce the same landing result.

example_games.title: Super Mario Bros.
example_games.note: Uses tuned gravity to shape jump arcs and falling commitment.

example_games.title: Portal
example_games.note: Uses gravity with spatial puzzle layouts and momentum transfer.
